title: Pin NumPy <2.0.0 before torch-npu installation for CANN 8.x compatibility

meta.refiner_reasoning: CANN 8.1.RC1 tbe/te packages reference np.float_ which was removed in NumPy 2.0+. Pinning numpy<2.0.0 before torch-npu installation prevents the import preflight AttributeError. Validated in GLM-OCR migration: 5 Phase 5 iterations, fix resolved on iteration 1 with FULL_PASS verification. No project code changes needed — purely a dependency version constraint.

problem_description: CANN 8.1.RC1 tbe/te packages reference np.float_ which was deprecated in NumPy 1.x and removed entirely in NumPy 2.0+. When NumPy >=2.0 is installed in the venv (the default on Python 3.10+ via pip), importing tbe or te fails with AttributeError: 'numpy' has no attribute 'float_'. This blocks the entire NPU import preflight, causing the migration validation to fail before the server even launches.
rough_fix_approach: Pin numpy<2.0.0 in the project venv during Phase 2 (venv creation) BEFORE installing torch-npu or any CANN-dependent packages. The fix command is: pip install 'numpy<2.0.0'. This restores the np.float_ alias that CANN 8.1.RC1 expects. No code changes needed in the migrated project.

_asset_contents.rule.yaml:
title: Pin NumPy <2.0.0 before torch-npu installation for CANN 8.x compatibility
pattern: numpy[\s><=!~]*2[\d.]*
replacement: numpy<2.0.0
file_patterns: ["requirements*.txt", "*.txt", "setup.cfg", "pyproject.toml"]
target_roles: ["code_adapter"]
target_phases: ["phase_4_rule_migration"]
trigger_fingerprint: dependency_issue|numpy2_incompatible_with_cann8|cann-8.x|dependency-version-pinning|numpy-compatibility|tbe-te-import|torch-npu|cc1caad4eb9a
